style: Dry, full-bodied, high tannin, black fruit, oak, ageing potential. Deep ruby colour, structured and ageworthy.

conclusions: Ready to drink or age 5-15+ years depending on vintage/terroir

common_student_errors: Missing the relationship between structure and ageing potential
Ignoring oak as a positive structural element

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Tannin and acidity are the same thing
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Tannin is structure and texture on palate; acidity is a taste sensation. Both contribute to Cabernet's ageability

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Higher alcohol always means better quality
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Alcohol provides body, but balance of tannin, acidity, and fruit determines quality

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Oak in red wine is always visible
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Well-integrated oak supports structure without being obvious; obvious oak suggests poor integration

coaching_hints: How does tannin texture feel on the palate?
Can you assess readiness for drinking based on tannin ripeness?

causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Full tannin and structure support ageing

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Black fruit (not red fruit); higher alcohol; full body
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Blackcurrant and plum rather than cherry

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Tannin integration; oak spice softens; secondary complexity develops
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Structure becomes more elegant with age
